title: Heapify the Array while Preserving the Uniqueness of Elements

statement.markdown:
Given an array, build a max-heap while preserving the uniqueness of elements in the heap.

A max-heap is a complete binary tree where the parent node is always greater than or equal to its child nodes.

The array has unique elements.

Return 1 if the array can be built into a valid max-heap. Otherwise, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the size of the array, and the second line contains the elements of the array separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: 1 if the array can be built into a valid max-heap. Otherwise, -1.
statement.notes: The array is guaranteed to have unique elements, and the size of the array is at least 1.

examples.explanation: This can be built into a valid max-heap: [4, 3, 2, 1].
This cannot be built into a valid max-heap because the array will have duplicate elements.

hints.text: First, check if the array can be built into a heap without any duplicates.
If no duplicates are found, use a max-heap library (e.g., priority_queue in C++) or implement a max-heap from scratch.
Finally, check if the built max-heap satisfies the heap property.

editorial.title: Building a Valid Max-Heap with Uniqueness
editorial.intuition: To preserve the uniqueness of elements, we need to check if the array contains any duplicates before building the max-heap.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to sort the array and then check if the max-heap property is satisfied.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a max-heap data structure that automatically handles uniqueness.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves checking for duplicates and then building a max-heap while preserving the uniqueness of elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: To prove the correctness of the optimal solution, we need to show that the built max-heap satisfies the heap property and that no duplicates are present.
editorial.dryRun: To perform a dry run, we can manually build a max-heap from the given array and verify that it satisfies the heap property.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for duplicates before building the max-heap
Not preserving the uniqueness of elements in the heap
